LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF WENDY OAKES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED ON APRIL 18, 2013, AFTER RECEIVING DISCOVERY RESPONSES, ADMISSIONS, AND DOCUMENTS FROM DEFENDANT ON JANUARY 28, AND FEBRUARY 12, 2013. PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER PUBLIC UTILITY CODE 5101 ET SEQ, RELATES TO THE SAME GENERAL SET OF FACTS AS PLAINTIFF'S BREACH OF CONTRACT AND INTENTIONAL TORT CAUSES OF ACTION. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT MUST BE FILED AND SERVED BY MAY 15, 2013 USING THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM. THE JUDICIAL ARBITRATION ON MAY 28, 2013 IS VACATED. DEPARTMENT 610 WILL SET A NEW DATE FOR THE JUDICIAL ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO AND COMPLIANCE WITH DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SET ONE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO AND COMPLIANCE WITH DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SET ONE IS DENIED. THE STATEMENTS SHOWING BALANCES IN THE ACCOUNT ON ANY GIVEN DATE WERE RESPONSIVE TO PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT SHE HAD THE FUNDS TO MAKE PAYMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE IRRELEVANT MATTERS FROM PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING SENTIMENTAL VALUE AND DENIED AS TO THE OTHER ALLEGATIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF SENTIMENTAL VALUE ARE NOT RECOVERABLE IN THIS CASE. THE REMAINING ALLEGATIONS SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: michael o'connell, esq., 415.678.1984, for defendant celtic moving & storage, inc.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted, with 10 days leave to amend. Ms. Oakes has not pled facts to show malice, oppression or fraud to support her exemplary damages claim under Pub. Util. Code , 5142(h). (Civ. Code 3294(c).) Ms. Oakes is granted leave to amend, only if she can do so good in faith, to allege facts to show that Celtic Moving & Storage Inc. acted with the requisite malice, oppression or fraud. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: michael o'connell, esq., 415.678.1984, for defendant celtic moving & storage, inc.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. Ms. Oakes has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for violation of the Household Goods Carrier Act. (Pub. Util. Code 5113, 5142(h).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Michael J. O'Connell, Esq. of the Law Offices of Michael J. O'Connell, Tel. 415.678.1984, for defendant and moving party;
Vadim Yaport, Esq. of Lvovich Volchegursky Szucksko, Tel. 45.392.2560, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Answer To Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One is granted. Within 10 days from the date of this Order, Plaintiff will provide Defendant with the account numbers and location of the Wells Fargo accounts and will identify with specificity the cashiers check identified in her earlier response to Interrogatory no. 19. Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar May-10-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anne Yarovoy, Esq. and Vadim Yaport, Esq. of Lvovich Volchegursky Szucsko, Tel. 415.392.2560, for plaintiff and moving party;
Michael J. O'Connell, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael O'Connell, Tel. 415.678.1984, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification as follows: PLAINTIFF WENDY OAKES' Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is granted, for good cause shown. Plaintiff's motion was timely filed on April 18, 2013, after receiving discovery responses, admissions, and documents from Defendant on January 28, and February 12, 2013. Plaintiff's proposed cause of action under Public Utility Code 5101 et seq, relates to the same general set of facts as Plaintiff's breach of contract and intentional tort causes of action. The amended complaint must be filed and served by May 15, 2013 using the Judicial Council form. The Judicial Arbitration on May 28, 2013 is vacated. Department 610 will set a new date for the judicial arbitration. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANNE YAROVOY, ESQ., LVOVICH VOLCHEGURSKY SZUCSKO, 415.392.2560, FOR PLAINTIFF WENDY OAKES; No other appearances.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANNE YAROVOY, ESQ., LVOVICH VOLCHEGURSKY SZUCSKO, 415.392.2560, FOR PLAINTIFF WENDY OAKES; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE irrelevant matters from plaintiff's complaint is granted without leave to amend as to the allegations regarding sentimental value and denied as to the other allegations sought to be stricken. Damages for loss of sentimental value are not recoverable in this case. The remaining allegations sought to be stricken are adequately pled. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
